info.name: Auto API - sanitized HTTP collection
info.description: Sanitized Postman HTTP collection for the Auto API portfolio project. The collection contains safe local test data only. WebSocket requests cannot be reliably imported through Postman Collection v2.1 JSON as true WebSocket tabs, so the collection contains the HTTP token endpoint and a folder description with manual WebSocket instructions.

item.name: 00 Setup / public
item.description: Before Postman: create admin superuser and seller using shell commands from the ChatGPT answer. Then login admin and create manager through API.

request.description: Requires superuser/admin token. If manager already exists, login manager below.
If registration creates inactive buyer, activate via shell/email token.
Use the activation token from the registration email/logs. This activates a newly registered user. For local testing, copy the token into the `activation_token` environment variable.
Starts password recovery. The response is intentionally generic, even when the email is not registered.
Use the recovery token from the recovery email/logs. For local testing, copy the token into the `recovery_token` environment variable.
Set blacklist_user_id to existing buyer id.

exec: pm.test('403 forbidden for this role', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(403); });
pm.test('403 forbidden for this role', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(403); });

item.name: 07 WebSocket helper

request.description: Run this request before opening the WebSocket tab. It saves `socket_token` to both collection variables and environment variables. The actual WebSocket request must be created manually in Postman: New → WebSocket.

exec: console.log('socket_token saved to environment and collection variables');

item.description:
Postman imports WebSocket requests from collection JSON as ordinary HTTP GET requests in many cases.

Do not add a fake GET request here.

Correct manual setup in Postman:
1. Run `Seller create socket token`.
2. Click `New` → `WebSocket`.
3. Paste: `ws://localhost:8000/api/chat/{{listing_id}}/?token={{socket_token}}`.
4. Make sure the selected environment is `Auto Local - sanitized`.
5. Click `Connect`.
6. Send JSON: `{ "action": "send_message", "body": "Hello from Postman" }`.

Important: the protocol must be `ws://`, not `http://`, and the request type in Postman must be WebSocket, not HTTP GET.
